articles.title: 'Toxic gases, carcinogens': Tracy medical warehouse fire raises air quality concerns as smoke billows
articles.description: San Joaquin County officials are warning residents about potential health risks as thick black smoke from the Tracy medical warehouse fire continues to billow on Friday. County public health officials said respiratory irritants, toxic gases, carcinogens, and "at least one neurotoxin" are present in the smoke and could impact nearby communities downwind.

articles.title: Kyrgyzstan: Heatwave - Early Action Protocol Annual Report (MDRKG016), January - December 2025
articles.description: Country: Kyrgyzstan Source: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies Please refer to the attached file. The IFRC Disaster Response Emergency Fund (DREF) has allocated CHF 499’151 for the implementation anticipatory actions to reduce and mitigate the impact of Heat Wave in Kyrgyzstan. This Early Action Protocol includes an allocation of CHF 239’078 to preposition stock and undertake annual readiness activities in order to implement early actions, if and when the trigger is reached. The early actions to be conducted have been pre-agreed with the National Society and are described in the Early Action Protocol summary. This report summarizes the annual readiness and prepositioning activities executed in the reporting period. The report also includes changes and updates made to the initially agreed plan. From 1 September 2024 to 31 August 2025, the Kyrgyz Red Crescent Society, with technical support from the German Red Cross and funding from the IFRC Disaster Relief Emergency Fund (DREF), implemented the Early Action Protocol on Heat Wave (EAP for HW). The main objective of this protocol was to reduce the negative impact of the abnormally hot weather on vulnerable populations of targeted areas in Kyrgyzstan, including large families, children in orphanages, and older people living in care centres

articles.title: Alaska’s glaciers have a startling response to rising temperatures
articles.description: Alaska’s glaciers are proving to be highly sensitive to warming temperatures. Using radar satellites to monitor more than 3,000 glaciers, researchers found that every 1°C (1.8°F) increase in average summer temperature extends glacier melting by about three weeks. The study also revealed that intense heat waves can strip away up to 28% more protective snow cover, exposing ice much earlier than normal and accelerating ice loss.

articles.title: The missing notebooks that solved a 55-million-year-old fossil mystery
articles.description: A spectacular fossil fish discovered on a remote cliff in New Zealand nearly 30 years ago has finally revealed its full story thanks to an unexpected discovery: the original collector’s long-lost field notebooks. The 1.2-meter fossil, preserved in stunning three-dimensional detail, belonged to an ancient tarpon-like predator that cruised New Zealand waters about 55 million years ago.

articles.title: A pod of dolphins greets tourists in Limasawa Island
articles.description: CONSOLACION, Cebu – Imagine being welcomed by dolphins on your way to an island getaway. That’s exactly what happened during Elaine Alcala’s trip: spotting a pod of dolphins as they made their way to Limasawa Island in Southern Leyte. Alcala said the experience felt surreal, especially since it was her first time seeing dolphins up close. “It felt unreal. It was my first time seeing a pod of dolphins up close, and some were even swimming under the boat. They were so cute,” she shared. articles.title: Taal Volcano sulfur dioxide emission drops sharply
articles.description: LUCENA CITY — Taal Volcano’s sulfur dioxide (SO2) emissions have significantly decreased after several days of elevated activity, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) reported. In its 5 a.m. bulletin on Friday, Phivolcs said only 188 metric tons (MT) of SO2 were emitted from the volcano’s main crater over the past 24 hours.

articles.title: Mindanao aftershocks near 3,900 — Phivolcs
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The number of aftershocks from the magnitude 7.8 offshore earthquake that struck Mindanao on June 8 has climbed to 3,860 as of 9 a.m. Friday, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) said. The agency reported that of the total, 966 aftershocks have been plotted, while 64 were felt by residents in affected areas. The recorded aftershocks ranged in magnitude from 1.2 to 6.4. articles.title: Philippines: PHL: Earthquake - 06-2026 - 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake in Maasim, Sarangani #3 (2026-06-11)
articles.description: Country: Philippines Sources: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies, Philippine Red Cross Please refer to the attached file. Description At 7:37 AM on 8 June 2026, a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck approximately 32 km south 04° west of Maasim, Sarangani, at a depth of 33 kilometers, affecting several areas in Mindanao. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) recorded the strongest ground shaking at Intensity VII (Destructive) in General Santos City, with expected aftershocks and reported damages. DOST-PHIVOLCS issued Tsunami Information No. 1 on 08 June 2026 at 7:37 AM, warning that coastal areas may experience tsunami wave heights of more than one meter above normal tide levels, which may be higher in enclosed bays and straits. Communities in Sarangani, Davao Occidental, Tawi-Tawi, Sulu, Basilan, Zamboanga del Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, Sultan Kudarat, and South Cotabato were strongly advised to immediately evacuate to safer areas. Based on reports from PRC Chapters, several structural damages were recorded. A total of two (2) structures totally collapsed in North Cotabato and General Santos City; four (4) structures sustained major damages in General Santos City, Davao City, and Sarangani; two (2) additional structures collapsed in General Santos City; four (4) structures sustained minor damages in Davao Oriental and Davao City; and one (1) incident of fallen debris was reported in Davao del Sur. Damaged structures included municipal halls, gyms, and commercial establishments. Following the magnitude 7.8 earthquake in Sarangani, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. ordered the suspension of classes at all levels across affected areas in Mindanao until further notice. STATE OF CALAMITY: City of General Santos: City-wide (Resolution No. 676 s. 2026) Municipality of Malapatan, Sarangani (Resolution No. 335 s. 2026 as of June 8, 2026) Municipality of Glan, Sarangani (Resolution No. 24-0809 s. 2026 as of June 9, 2026) Municipality of Jose Abad Santos, Davao Occidental as of June 10, 2026 Davao Occidental: Province-wide (Resolution No. 45-2025 as of June 9, 2026) As of the latest PHIVOLCS report released today, a total of 2,889 aftershocks have been recorded following the magnitude 7.8 earthquake in Sarangani. Of these, 755 aftershocks were plotted/located , while 58 were felt by residents in affected areas. The recorded aftershocks ranged from magnitude 1.2 to 6.4.
